tossups.question: This artist’s The Upper Room is an installation of paintings of rhesus macaque monkeys. In 1999, Dennis Heiner, who accused this artist of “Catholic bashing,” smeared white paint over one of his canvases. This artist was inspired by the Jab Jab participants in the Trinidad and Tobago Carnival for his “blue” studies. A work by this artist features collaged images of Stephen Lawrence, a black British teenager who was murdered in a racially-motivated crime. (*) This artist riffed on Great Britain’s Union Jack flag, replacing its colors with those of the Pan-African tricolor. A caged and weeping woman in a red dress is the central figure of this artist’s No Woman No Cry.In 1996, a mixed-media painting by this artist drew controversy for its inclusion of pornographic images in an image of a religious icon. For 10 points, name this Young British Artist who controversially included elephant dung in his painting of The Holy Virgin Mary.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist's The Upper Room is an installation of paintings of rhesus macaque monkeys. In 1999, Dennis Heiner, who accused this artist of "Catholic bashing," smeared white paint over one of his canvases. This artist was inspired by the Jab Jab participants in the Trinidad and Tobago Carnival for his "blue" studies. A work by this artist features collaged images of Stephen Lawrence, a black British teenager who was murdered in a racially-motivated crime. (*) This artist riffed on Great Britain's Union Jack flag, replacing its colors with those of the Pan-African tricolor. A caged and weeping woman in a red dress is the central figure of this artist's No Woman No Cry.In 1996, a mixed-media painting by this artist drew controversy for its inclusion of pornographic images in an image of a religious icon. For 10 points, name this Young British Artist who controversially included elephant dung in his painting of The Holy Virgin Mary.

tossups.question: The Clothworkers' Company commissioned this artist to turn his The Caged Bird's Song into a tapestry for the 2017 Weaving Magic exhibition at London's National Gallery. A bead-filled multimedia work by this artist features stars of Blaxploitation films with afros clumsily added to their heads. Responding to a "kid [who] had been killed by white racists," this artist adopted a Bob Marley title for his painting of a weeping Doreen Lawrence. Dennis Heiner threw white paint onto the most famous painting of this artist of (*) No Woman No Cry. Like his friend Peter Doig, he now lives on Trinidad. A work that this artist made for Sensation featured flies constructed from vaginas cut out of magazines behind the title religious figure. For 10 points, name this British artist whose The Holy Virgin Mary attracted criticism from Rudy Giuliani for being made from elephant dung.
tossups.question_sanitized: The Clothworkers' Company commissioned this artist to turn his The Caged Bird's Song into a tapestry for the 2017 Weaving Magic exhibition at London's National Gallery. A bead-filled multimedia work by this artist features stars of Blaxploitation films with afros clumsily added to their heads. Responding to a "kid [who] had been killed by white racists," this artist adopted a Bob Marley title for his painting of a weeping Doreen Lawrence. Dennis Heiner threw white paint onto the most famous painting of this artist of (*) No Woman No Cry. Like his friend Peter Doig, he now lives on Trinidad. A work that this artist made for Sensation featured flies constructed from vaginas cut out of magazines behind the title religious figure. For 10 points, name this British artist whose The Holy Virgin Mary attracted criticism from Rudy Giuliani for being made from elephant dung.

tossups.question: This artist used a technique he learned while studying cave paintings in Zimbabwe to paint a vase-holding monkey surrounded by dots and the words “Sex,” “Money,” and “Drugs.” A lithograph by this artist has the words “R.I.P. Stephen Lawrence” superimposed over the silhouette of a previous portrait of his. The Tate Gallery faced controversy for purchasing an installation by this artist while he served on their Board of Trustees; that installation features (*) Mono Oro at the head of a room surrounded by 12 paintings of animals representing the apostles. This artist of The Upper Room cut out images from porn magazines to create cherubs for a painting deemed by Rudy Giuliani to be “blasphemous” for depicting the Virgin Mary made out of elephant dung. For 10 points, name this artist of No Woman No Cry and The Holy Virgin Mary, a Young British Artist who resides in Trinidad.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist used a technique he learned while studying cave paintings in Zimbabwe to paint a vase-holding monkey surrounded by dots and the words "Sex," "Money," and "Drugs." A lithograph by this artist has the words "R.I.P. Stephen Lawrence" superimposed over the silhouette of a previous portrait of his. The Tate Gallery faced controversy for purchasing an installation by this artist while he served on their Board of Trustees; that installation features (*) Mono Oro at the head of a room surrounded by 12 paintings of animals representing the apostles. This artist of The Upper Room cut out images from porn magazines to create cherubs for a painting deemed by Rudy Giuliani to be "blasphemous" for depicting the Virgin Mary made out of elephant dung. For 10 points, name this artist of No Woman No Cry and The Holy Virgin Mary, a Young British Artist who resides in Trinidad.

tossups.question: This artist riffed on traditional Catalan caganer figurines for a pair of sculptures called Silver Moon and Blue Moon, which were exhibited in Berlin as part of his Blue Rider exhibit there. One of his early works featured "7 Bitches Tossing their Pussies before" the other title objects. Over 100 of his watercolor busts of men and women with crazy hairstyles were exhibited at his one-man show Afro Muses. This artist, who works from Port of Spain in Trinidad, incorporated baby teeth and some of his own hair into a sculpture for which the primary material was collected from the London Zoo. One of his paintings includes collaged images of cut-out (*) buttocks of porn stars, which stand in for angels. This first black artist to win the Turner Prize sparked a controversy when Rudy Giuliani threatened to cut funding for the Brooklyn Museum due to the sacrilegious nature of that painting. For 10 points, name this artist of Shithead who used elephant dung to create a 1996 painting of the Holy Virgin Mary.

bonuses.leadin: One work commemorating this event recontextualizes the photography of Khadija Saye (“kuh-DEE-juh SAH-yeh”), a 24-year-old artist who died as a result of it. For 10 points each,
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: One work commemorating this event recontextualizes the photography of Khadija Saye ("kuh-DEE-juh SAH-yeh"), a 24-year-old artist who died as a result of it. For 10 points each,
parts: Name this event commemorated by a 24-minute Steve McQueen-directed documentary exhibited at the Serpentine Gallery and a gallery-sized mural at the Tate Britain.
That mural, Requiem, was painted by this Manchester-born artist of The Upper Room, a series of 13 paintings of rhesus macaques.
For his work, including his Stephen Lawrence tribute No Woman No Cry, Ofili won the 1996 iteration of this annual award, which is given out at the Tate Britain in alternating years.
parts_sanitized: Name this event commemorated by a 24-minute Steve McQueen-directed documentary exhibited at the Serpentine Gallery and a gallery-sized mural at the Tate Britain.
That mural, Requiem, was painted by this Manchester-born artist of The Upper Room, a series of 13 paintings of rhesus macaques.
For his work, including his Stephen Lawrence tribute No Woman No Cry, Ofili won the 1996 iteration of this annual award, which is given out at the Tate Britain in alternating years.

bonuses.leadin: The lithograph version of this painting has an orange border with sperm cells and a fetus in the bottom left corner. For 10 points each:
parts: Give the common name of this Edvard Munch painting depicting a nude woman with long black hair and a red halo above her head. Munch, while not a Christian, gave this work a religious name.
In this Mannerist painting, the Virgin Mary holds a large infant Jesus in her lap. It takes its name from the enlarged physical feature that the artist, Parmigianino, gave to Mary.

This Trinidadian-British artist faced controversy in 1999 for his work The Holy Virgin Mary, which depicted a Black Madonna made out of elephant dung, a material which he also used in his painting No Woman No Cry.
answers: Madonna of the Long Neck [Accept The Madonna with the Long Neckor Madonna and Child with Angels and St. Jerome; or La Madonna del Collo Lungo]
answers_sanitized: Madonna [accept Loving Woman or Woman Making Love]
Madonna of the Long Neck [Accept The Madonna with the Long Neckor Madonna and Child with Angels and St. Jerome; or La Madonna del Collo Lungo]

This Trinidadian-British artist faced controversy in 1999 for his work The Holy Virgin Mary, which depicted a Black Madonna made out of elephant dung, a material which he also used in his painting No Woman No Cry.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, answer some questions about the adventures of Nicholas Serota.
parts: Serota directed this organization from 1988 to 2017. Named after a sugar magnate, it runs four British museums including the [this] Modern in London.
Serota controversially bought two stolen paintings by this British artist directly from the mafia. This artist of Rain, Steam and Speed also names an art prize which Serota chaired.

In 2005, it was revealed that Serota had broken nonprofit regulations by buying an exhibition called The Upper Room from this artist of No Woman No Cry, who sat on the Tate’s board of trustees at the time.
